function solve(chunks: string[], queries: string[]): number[] {
    // Step 1: Construct s
    let s = chunks.join('');

    // Step 2: Preprocessing s
    
    // 2a. Replace any sequence of non-letters ([^a-z]) with a single space.
    // This handles spaces, punctuation, etc.
    // Note: The hyphen '-' is a letter/hyphen allowed character, so [^a-z] excludes it.
    // If the original chunk contained '-', it will remain untouched unless it was adjacent to another non-letter character.
    // Wait, if we use [^a-z], '-' remains. This is correct for Step 2a.
    s = s.replace(/[^a-z-]/g, ' ');

    // 2b. Replace any sequence of two or more hyphens (--+) with a single space.
    s = s.replace(/-{2,}/g, ' ');

    // Step 3: Tokenization
    // Split by one or more spaces and filter out empty strings resulting from leading/trailing/multiple spaces.
    const words = s.split(/\s+/).filter(word => word.length > 0);

    // Step 4: Counting
    const wordCounts = new Map<string, number>();
    for (const word of words) {
        wordCounts.set(word, (wordCounts.get(word) || 0) + 1);
    }

    // Step 5: Result Generation
    const ans: number[] = [];
    for (const query of queries) {
        ans.push(wordCounts.get(query) || 0);
    }

    return ans;
}
